Waiver of Federal Fund Limitation for the Drug-Free Communities Support Program, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–103, div. P, title I, § 161136 Stat. 801Mar. 15, 2022“(a).—In GeneralSubject to subsection (b), if the Administrator of the Drug-Free Communities Support Program determines that, as a result of the public health emergency declared pursuant to section 319 of the Public Health Service Act () with respect to COVID–19, an eligible coalition is unable to raise the amount of non-Federal funds, including in-kind contributions, agreed to be raised by the coalition for fiscal year 2020, 2021, or 2022 under an agreement entered into with the Administrator pursuant to paragraph (1)(A) or (3) of section 1032(b) of the Anti-Drug Abuse Act of 1988 (), the Administrator may, notwithstanding such paragraphs, provide to the eligible coalition the grant or renewal grant, as applicable, for that fiscal year only in an amount—42 U.S.C. 247d21 U.S.C. 1532(b)“(1)with respect to an initial grant or renewal grant described under paragraph (1)(A) or (3)(A) of such section, that exceeds the amount of non-Federal funds raised by the eligible coalition, including in-kind contributions, for that fiscal year;“(2)with respect to a renewal grant described under paragraph (3)(D)(i) of such section, that exceeds 125 percent of the amount of non-Federal funds raised by the eligible coalition, including in-kind contributions, for that fiscal year; and“(3)with respect to a renewal grant described under paragraph (3)(D)(ii) of such section, that exceeds 150 percent of the amount of non-Federal funds raised by the eligible coalition, including in-kind contributions, for that fiscal year.“(b).—LimitationThe Administrator may not provide a grant or renewal grant to an eligible coalition in an amount exceeding the amount of funds initially agreed to be provided by the Administrator under the applicable agreement.”

children.heading: Application
children.label: 1 Application
children.text: The coalition shall submit an application to the Administrator in accordance with .section 1533(a)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: Youth.

children.label: ii
children.text: Parents.

children.label: iii
children.text: Businesses.

children.label: iv
children.text: The media.

children.label: v
children.text: Schools.

children.label: vi
children.text: Organizations serving youth.

children.label: vii
children.text: Law enforcement.

children.label: viii
children.text: Religious or fraternal organizations.

children.label: ix
children.text: Civic and volunteer groups.

children.label: x
children.text: Health care professionals.

children.label: xi
children.text: State, local, or tribal governmental agencies with expertise in the field of substance use and misuse (including, if applicable, the State authority with primary authority for substance use and misuse).

children.label: xii
children.text: Other organizations involved in reducing substance use and misuse.

children.label: i
children.text: the Federal Government; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the government of the appropriate State and political subdivision thereof or the governing body or an Indian tribe (as that term is defined in ).section 5304(e) of title 25

children.heading: Representation
children.label: C Representation
children.text: An individual who is a member of the coalition may serve on the coalition as a representative of not more than 1 category listed under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: that the representatives of the coalition have worked together on substance use and misuse reduction initiatives, which, at a minimum, includes initiatives that target drugs referenced in , for a period of not less than 6 months, acting through entities such as task forces, subcommittees, or community boards; andsection 1523(9)(A) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: substantial participation from volunteer leaders in the community involved (especially in cooperation with individuals involved with youth such as parents, teachers, coaches, youth workers, and members of the clergy).

children.label: A
children.text: have as its principal mission the reduction of substance use and misuse, which, at a minimum, includes the use and abuse of drugs referenced in , in a comprehensive and long-term manner, with a primary focus on youth in the community;section 1523(9)(A) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: describe and document the nature and extent of the substance use and misuse problem, which, at a minimum, includes the use and abuse of drugs referenced in , in the community;section 1523(9)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: provide a description of substance use and misuse prevention and treatment programs and activities, which, at a minimum, includes programs and activities relating to the use and abuse of drugs referenced in , in existence at the time of the grant application; andsection 1523(9)(A) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: identify substance use and misuse programs and service gaps, which, at a minimum, includes programs and gaps relating to the use and abuse of drugs referenced in , in the community;section 1523(9)(A) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: develop a strategic plan to reduce substance use and misuse among youth, which, at a minimum, includes the use and abuse of drugs referenced in , in a comprehensive and long-term fashion; andsection 1523(9)(A) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: work to develop a consensus regarding the priorities of the community to combat substance use and misuse among youth, which, at a minimum, includes the use and abuse of drugs referenced in .section 1523(9)(A) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: a nonprofit organization; or

children.label: II
children.text: an entity that the Administrator determines to be appropriate; or

children.label: ii
children.text: part of, or is associated with, an established legal entity;

children.label: B
children.text: receives financial support (including, in the discretion of the Administrator, in-kind contributions) from non-Federal sources; and

children.label: C
children.text: has a strategy to solicit substantial financial support from non-Federal sources to ensure that the coalition and the programs operated by the coalition are self-sustaining.

children.label: i
children.text: consistent with common indicators and evaluation protocols established by the Administrator; and

children.label: ii
children.text: approved by the Administrator;

children.label: i
children.text: for an initial grant under this part, an initial benchmark survey of drug use among youth (or use local surveys or performance measures available or accessible in the community at the time of the grant application); and

children.label: ii
children.text: biennial surveys (or incorporate local surveys in existence at the time of the evaluation) to measure the progress and effectiveness of the coalition; and

children.label: i
children.text: in gathering data related to substance use and misuse among youth; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in evaluating the effectiveness of community anti-drug coalitions.

children.heading: Additional criteria
children.label: 7 Additional criteria
children.text: The Director shall not impose any eligibility criteria on new applicants or renewal grantees not provided in this subchapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (iv), for a fiscal year, the Administrator may grant to an eligible coalition under this paragraph, an amount not to exceed the amount of non-Federal funds raised by the coalition, including in-kind contributions, for that fiscal year.

children.heading: Suspension of grants
children.label: ii Suspension of grants
children.text: If such grant recipient fails to continue to meet the criteria specified in subsection (a), the Administrator may suspend the grant, after providing written notice to the grant recipient and an opportunity to appeal.

children.heading: Renewal grants
children.label: iii Renewal grants
children.text: Subject to clause (iv), the Administrator may award a renewal grant to a grant recipient under this subparagraph for each fiscal year following the fiscal year for which an initial grant is awarded, in an amount not to exceed the amount of non-Federal funds raised by the coalition, including in-kind contributions, for that fiscal year, during the 4-year period following the period of the initial grant.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: iv Limitation
children.text: The amount of a grant award under this subparagraph may not exceed $125,000 for a fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the Administrator may, with respect to a community, make a grant to 1 eligible coalition that represents that community.

children.label: I
children.text: the eligible coalitions demonstrate that the coalitions are collaborating with one another; and

children.label: II
children.text: each of the coalitions has independently met the requirements set forth in subsection (a).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In addition to awarding grants under paragraph (1), to stimulate the development of coalitions in sparsely populated and rural areas, the Administrator, in consultation with the Advisory Commission, may award a grant in accordance with this section to a coalition that represents a county with a population that does not exceed 30,000 individuals. In awarding a grant under this paragraph, the Administrator may waive any requirement under subsection (a) if the Administrator considers that waiver to be appropriate.

children.heading: Matching requirement
children.label: ii Matching requirement
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (C), for a fiscal year, the Administrator may grant to an eligible coalition under this paragraph, an amount not to exceed the amount of non-Federal funds raised by the coalition, including in-kind contributions, for that fiscal year.

children.heading: Suspension of grants
children.label: iii Suspension of grants
children.text: If such grant recipient fails to continue to meet any criteria specified in subsection (a) that has not been waived by the Administrator pursuant to clause (i), the Administrator may suspend the grant, after providing written notice to the grant recipient and an opportunity to appeal.

children.heading: Renewal grants
children.label: B Renewal grants
children.text: The Administrator may award a renewal grant to an eligible coalition that is a grant recipient under this paragraph for each fiscal year following the fiscal year for which an initial grant is awarded, in an amount not to exceed the amount of non-Federal funds raised by the coalition, including in-kind contributions, during the 4-year period following the period of the initial grant.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: i Amount
children.text: The amount of a grant award under this paragraph shall not exceed $125,000 for a fiscal year.

children.heading: Awards
children.label: ii Awards
children.text: With respect to a county referred to in subparagraph (A), the Administrator may award a grant under this section to not more than 1 eligible coalition that represents the county.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (F), the Administrator may award an additional grant under this paragraph to an eligible coalition awarded a grant under paragraph (1) or (2) for any first fiscal year after the end of the 4-year period following the period of the initial grant under paragraph (1) or (2), as the case may be.

children.heading: Scope of grants
children.label: B Scope of grants
children.text: A coalition awarded a grant under paragraph (1) or (2), including a renewal grant under such paragraph, may not be awarded another grant under such paragraph, and is eligible for an additional grant under this section only under this paragraph.

children.heading: No priority for applications
children.label: C No priority for applications
children.text: The Administrator may not afford a higher priority in the award of an additional grant under this paragraph than the Administrator would afford the applicant for the grant if the applicant were submitting an application for an initial grant under paragraph (1) or (2) rather than an application for a grant under this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: For the first and second fiscal years of the 4-fiscal-year period, the amount of the non-Federal funds, including in-kind contributions, raised by the coalition for the applicable fiscal year is not less than 125 percent of the amount awarded.

children.label: ii
children.text: For the third and fourth fiscal years of the 4-fiscal-year period, the amount of the non-Federal funds, including in-kind contributions, raised by the coalition for the applicable fiscal year is not less than 150 percent of the amount awarded.

children.heading: Suspension
children.label: E Suspension
children.text: If a grant recipient under this paragraph fails to continue to meet the criteria specified in subsection (a), the Administrator may suspend the grant, after providing written notice to the grant recipient and an opportunity to appeal.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: F Limitation
children.text: The amount of a grant award under this paragraph may not exceed $125,000 for a fiscal year.

children.heading: Process for suspension
children.label: 4 Process for suspension
children.text: A grantee shall not be suspended or terminated under paragraph (1)(A)(ii), (2)(A)(iii), or (3)(E) unless that grantee is afforded a fair, timely, and independent appeal prior to such suspension or termination.

children.heading: Treatment of funds for coalitions representing certain organizations
children.label: c Treatment of funds for coalitions representing certain organizations
children.text: Funds appropriated for the substance use and misuse activities of a coalition that includes a representative of the Bureau of Indian Affairs, the Indian Health Service, or a tribal government agency with expertise in the field of substance use and misuse may be counted as non-Federal funds raised by the coalition for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Priority in awarding grants
children.label: d Priority in awarding grants
children.text: In awarding grants under subsection (b)(1)(A)(i), priority shall be given to a coalition serving economically disadvantaged areas.
